mnemonics: TAMP vs. MAMP (Traditional Agile Modern Practices): Think of TAMP as a rigid stamp for documents (waterfall) and MAMP as a flexible map (agile) that you can redraw.
keyPoints: Traditional (Waterfall) is sequential and plan-driven, with rigid phases and late testing.
Modern (Agile/Scrum) is iterative and adaptive, with incremental delivery and continuous feedback.
The core difference is in flexibility: Traditional resists change, while modern embraces it.
commonMistakes: Assuming Agile means no planning or documentation; it involves just as much but is done incrementally.
Applying a purely traditional approach to a project with high uncertainty and changing requirements.
Believing the practices are mutually exclusive; hybrid approaches often combine elements of both.
